hypothesis: If graph neural networks use dynamic batching that sets padding targets from a data subset and fills batches to those targets, then the mean end-to-end training step time (batching plus gradient update) over long runs (e.g., 2 million steps) will be significantly lower than with static-constant, static-2N, or static-64 padding across batch sizes, datasets (QM9, AFLOW), and models (SchNet, PaiNN, MPEU) on an A100 GPU because dynamic batching reduces wasted computation from over-padding without incurring prohibitive recompilation overhead.
